steps.title: Support country-specific causes
steps.content: The student claims that factors specific to particular Western countries influenced the increase in age at first marriage. The best graph evidence would show the increase happening at different rates in different countries.

steps.title: Reject static comparisons
steps.content: Choice B focuses on sex differences within countries. Choice C discusses one country's pattern without directly comparing national trajectories. Choice D compares levels rather than rate of increase. A sharper rise in England and Wales than in the United States supports country-specific factors. The answer is A.
